intent: Orientation-only derived helper for staged retrieval. This helper never outranks fresh evidence or the shipped markdown sources.

supported_modes.use_when: the request is still early, vague, adjective-heavy, or mostly strategy language
supported_modes.goal: turn messy intent into grounded structure.

supported_modes.use_when: the request includes screenshots, existing UI, current DS docs, or “improve this” language
supported_modes.goal: identify strengths, weaknesses, missing foundations, and fix-first priorities.

supported_modes.use_when: the available context is strong enough to recommend principles, foundation priorities, and token direction
supported_modes.goal: recommend foundation-first direction without pretending the whole system is already defined.

supported_modes.use_when: the request asks what something is close to, what to borrow carefully, or which reference logic fits best
supported_modes.goal: turn comparison into explanation and safe borrowing guidance.

routing_rules.prompt_shape: intent-heavy wording, early brief fragments, strategy notes, or adjective-heavy asks
screenshots, current UI, current DS docs, “improve this”, or “enhance this”
recurring or scheduled DS review asks, repeated health checks, or prompts that explicitly point to `review-brief.md` or `review-log.md`
routing_rules.primary_mode: UI / DS Audit using the recurring-review shell

routing_rules.prompt_shape: URL-only or single-page asks such as “check this page”, “what looks healthy”, “what feels weak”, or “what should change first”
routing_rules.primary_mode: UI / DS Audit, even if the page is itself a design-system reference surface

routing_rules.prompt_shape: direct asks for principles, foundations, token direction, or what to stabilize first
named systems, “X or Y”, “what should we borrow”, or careful comparison asks

reference_cards.name: GOV.UK
reference_cards.best_used_for: public-service clarity and trust-heavy guidance
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: plain-language hierarchy and safe comprehension
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: content-first structure, clear form guidance, restrained interaction
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: extreme restraint for products that need stronger identity or denser workflows

reference_cards.name: USWDS
reference_cards.best_used_for: accessible public-sector patterns and stable service UI
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: accessible defaults and predictable system behavior
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: semantics, contrast discipline, service clarity
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: government tone in commercial or expressive contexts

reference_cards.name: Shopify Polaris
reference_cards.best_used_for: merchant operations and revenue-adjacent workflows
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: operational clarity for products, orders, settings, and decisions
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: state clarity, risk/value cues, management patterns
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: merchant logic for generic dashboards or consumer-first journeys

reference_cards.name: GitHub Primer
reference_cards.best_used_for: scalable system structure and design-to-code alignment
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: reusable primitives, documentation discipline, semantic consistency
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: token discipline, naming, adoption patterns
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: platform-scale abstraction before product patterns are stable

reference_cards.name: Atlassian Design System
reference_cards.best_used_for: product-scale consistency across teams and complex software surfaces
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: strong implementation support with mature documentation
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: contribution thinking, composability, foundation consistency
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: governance overhead that a smaller team cannot sustain

reference_cards.name: Salesforce Lightning
reference_cards.best_used_for: dense operational software and enterprise workflow support
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: structured patterns for heavy, repeated work
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: workflow clarity, data-heavy hierarchy, state handling
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: enterprise density for lightweight or brand-led products

reference_cards.name: Uber Base
reference_cards.best_used_for: internal/product workflow systems with practical operational gravity
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: pragmatic structure for task-heavy product surfaces
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: layout logic, dense pattern support, operational clarity
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: admin-heavy structure where discovery or emotion should lead

reference_cards.name: Visa Product Design System
reference_cards.best_used_for: trust-sensitive operational products that need workflow clarity, accessibility discipline, and polished implementation support without defaulting to pure platform logic
reference_cards.likely_archetype: hybrid-sensitive; strongest lean is Enterprise Workflow with bounded Developer Toolkit / Platform support traits
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: operational workflow patterns can feel trustworthy and well-governed when accessibility, content discipline, theming, and implementation support stay paired with repeated-job clarity
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: application layouts, dense task patterns, accessibility and content discipline, trust-bearing hierarchy, and the practical pairing of design guidance with code support
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: finance-brand polish, mature-enterprise tone, multi-framework breadth, or polished trust cues as proof that the product itself should now behave like a broader platform
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if Visa feels trusted and mature, separate what you actually need from it: stronger workflow clarity, stronger trust framing, or simply more polished enterprise tone

reference_cards.name: Airbnb Design Language System
reference_cards.best_used_for: brand-led systems with controlled expression and product polish
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: expression with strong system discipline
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: tone, shape language, cohesive perception
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: brand-led motion or softness where trust/workflow clarity should dominate

reference_cards.name: Ant Design
reference_cards.best_used_for: workflow-heavy business UI when teams need broad enterprise pattern coverage and tokenized theming flexibility
reference_cards.likely_archetype: hybrid-sensitive; strongest lean is Enterprise Workflow with bounded Developer Toolkit / Platform traits
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: dense business-software coverage plus theme architecture can accelerate operational UI without forcing a single visual formula
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: enterprise pattern coverage, data-heavy structure, state handling, and tokenized theming discipline
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: broad component coverage as proof of product fit, or default business UI tone for every workflow product
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if the team wants Ant because it feels complete, check whether they actually need workflow coverage first or are just reaching for maturity by default

reference_cards.name: Carbon
reference_cards.best_used_for: platform-scale system rigor when the product also carries real enterprise and operational gravity
reference_cards.likely_archetype: hybrid-sensitive; strongest lean is Developer Toolkit / Platform with real Enterprise Workflow gravity
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: ecosystem-level system building through disciplined foundations, accessibility rigor, and cross-product consistency
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: foundation discipline, accessibility verification, documentation structure, and multi-surface consistency thinking
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: IBM-scale formality, heavy governance, or platform-weight visual language before your own patterns and adoption scale justify it
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if Carbon feels “mature,” separate what you need from it: platform rigor, enterprise clarity, or simply better workflow patterns

reference_cards.name: PatternFly
reference_cards.best_used_for: operations-heavy enterprise products where task states, management flows, and dense admin structure matter more than platform prestige
reference_cards.likely_archetype: hybrid-sensitive; strongest lean is Enterprise Workflow with Developer Toolkit / Platform support traits
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: practical support for task-heavy management patterns, state visibility, and dense operational structure
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: management states, action hierarchy, dense information structure, and practical consistency across admin surfaces
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: infrastructure-heavy operational models for products that need stronger commerce logic, lighter foundations, or clearer brand tone
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if the team is split between Carbon and PatternFly, start from repeated operational jobs before comparing docs polish or ecosystem maturity

reference_cards.name: Clarity Design System
reference_cards.best_used_for: enterprise application work where inclusive defaults, implementation assets, and practical workflow clarity matter more than distinctive brand expression
reference_cards.likely_archetype: Enterprise Workflow with bounded Developer Toolkit / Platform traits
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: enterprise-first systems can keep inclusion and accessibility as a real foundation instead of a late compliance layer
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: inclusive defaults, accessibility-backed workflow patterns, public roadmap signals, and practical implementation support for enterprise teams
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: framework-shaped opinionation, enterprise tone, or heavier implementation assumptions when the product needs lighter foundations or more consumer-facing posture
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if Clarity feels “solid and accessible,” let it lead when day-to-day success still depends more on workflow clarity, review work, and inclusive defaults than on platform breadth

reference_cards.name: Elastic UI Framework
reference_cards.best_used_for: dense internal product surfaces that need composable implementation assets, theming support, and reusable patterns for data-rich workflows
reference_cards.likely_archetype: hybrid-sensitive; strongest lean is Developer Toolkit / Platform with real Enterprise Workflow gravity
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: a reusable implementation layer can stay paired with dense workflow patterns and theme discipline without pretending brand expression is the main job
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: composable patterns, theme-token setup, accessibility baselines, and documentation-backed support for dense internal product work
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: Elastic-shaped data-product assumptions, platform breadth, or observability-style density where the product reality is lighter or more narrowly task-shaped
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if EUI feels like the strongest library option, let it lead only when composable implementation breadth, theming, and cross-surface reuse now matter more than day-to-day workflow fit

reference_cards.name: Porsche Design System
reference_cards.best_used_for: brand-led system building where premium expression must stay controlled, portable, and implementation-aware
reference_cards.likely_archetype: Brand / Consumer / Industry-Specific with bounded Developer Toolkit / Platform traits
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: controlled premium expression can be systematized without giving up accessibility or implementation rigor
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: tone restraint, shape language, premium perception, theming discipline, and accessibility-backed implementation quality
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: luxury-brand cues, polished surface language, or premium symbolism in unrelated product contexts
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if the team says “make it feel more premium,” borrow Porsche for restraint and system discipline, not for direct visual transplantation

reference_cards.name: Gestalt
reference_cards.best_used_for: consumer-product systems that need coherent brand tone, reusable code, and accessible product guidance across many contributors
reference_cards.likely_archetype: Brand / Consumer / Industry-Specific with bounded Developer Toolkit / Platform traits
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: distinctive consumer-product tone can stay coherent at scale without giving up a usable implementation layer
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: cohesive tone, accessible product guidance, cross-team shared language, and restrained expression that supports product clarity
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: Pinterest-specific discovery cues, soft consumer mannerisms, or visible surface polish without matching product logic
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if Gestalt feels polished, borrow coherence and restraint before copying the visible consumer surface

reference_cards.name: Backpack
reference_cards.best_used_for: consumer-product systems where accessibility, localisation, and cross-disciplinary consistency matter across repeated high-volume journeys
reference_cards.likely_archetype: Brand / Consumer / Industry-Specific with bounded Developer Toolkit / Platform traits
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: accessibility, localisation, and content guidance can be first-class system inputs rather than add-ons behind a branded surface
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: localisation-aware content patterns, accessible journey support, collaborative product language, and friendly clarity across repeated user flows
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: travel-specific search or booking assumptions, friendly consumer tone, or Skyscanner-shaped journey logic in unrelated products
reference_cards.developer_guidance_cue: if Backpack feels “usable and friendly,” isolate whether that comes from localisation and accessibility discipline or from travel-product flow assumptions

reference_cards.name: Chakra UI
reference_cards.best_used_for: lightweight platform-style reuse and pragmatic implementation
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: approachable composability and reusable primitives
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: flexible primitives, scalable naming, implementation simplicity
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: assuming primitive flexibility solves unclear product patterns

reference_cards.name: shadcn/ui
reference_cards.best_used_for: fast product building with composable UI starting points
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: practical composability and implementation speed
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: building-block mindset, local ownership, flexible assembly
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: raw assembly speed without foundation/token discipline

reference_cards.name: Headless UI
reference_cards.best_used_for: interaction primitives and accessibility-aware composition
reference_cards.strongest_reusable_lesson: behavior-first primitives without heavy styling assumptions
reference_cards.borrow_carefully: interaction logic and accessibility-aware composition
reference_cards.do_not_copy_blindly: expecting headless primitives to replace product-level system decisions
